<!-- ZONE 1: high lexical overlap with input_source_alpha.md -->
Post-training quantization converts trained model weights from 32-bit floating-point to 8-bit integers. This reduces both the storage footprint and the inference latency on edge hardware. It is attractive because it requires no access to the original training pipeline or dataset.

<!-- ZONE 2: paraphrase of input_source_beta.md -->
Once training is complete, quantization shrinks the model by reducing the bit width of its parameters. Moving from FP32 representation to INT8 slashes memory usage by a factor of four and accelerates dense linear algebra on integer-capable processors.

<!-- ZONE 3: unrelated content -->
The 2024 Tour de France route included unprecedented gravel stages in the Vosges mountains. Riders on carbon-fiber road bikes faced puncture risks from sharp flint shards, forcing teams to carry tubeless sealant and spare wheels in team cars for the first time in decades.
